title: Numbers: Census and Camp
objective: Deepen biblical-theological understanding of numbers: census and camp and its place in redemptive history.

required_passages.text: Selected key passages for this unit segment, read in full during lesson delivery.

segments.audio_script: Today we study numbers: census and camp and trace God's covenant faithfulness.
Listen to the assigned passage and note covenant themes, divine actions, and human response.
We place this text in its historical setting, canonical location, and literary flow.
We analyze theological meaning, covenant development, and connections to the wider biblical story.
Key themes include God's holiness, covenant faithfulness, judgment and mercy, and redemptive purpose.
Review question: What was the primary purpose of the census in Numbers 1? Answer: to count all men in Israel aged twenty and above who were able to serve in the army.
segments.correct_answer: to count all men able to serve in the army, twenty years old and above
acceptable_variants: a military census of fighting-age men
numbering the men of war in each tribe
counting israel's soldiers before the march to canaan

segments.audio_script: Carry this lesson into prayer, worship, and careful Scripture reading as we continue the course.

vocabulary.definition: The counting of Israel's fighting men in Numbers 1 and again in Numbers 26; both censuses frame the wilderness generation that died and the new generation that would enter Canaan
The tribe set apart for tabernacle service rather than military service; they were not included in the military census but counted separately to serve as guardians of the sanctuary
